channel.title: NYT > Most Recent Headlines
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Iran War Live Updates: Trump’s Hormuz Deadline Hangs Over the Middle East
item.description: President Trump has threatened to destroy Iran’s bridges and power plants on Tuesday night Eastern time if it does not open the Strait of Hormuz. There have been no public signs of a diplomatic breakthrough.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

media:content.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times

item.title: How to Build a Rest Stop for Delivery Workers in a Hurry
item.description: A shelter for delivery workers was proposed years ago. Then Mayor Zohran Mamdani decided that it needed to be finished within his first 100 days in office.
item.dc:creator: Christopher Maag and Graham Dickie

item.title: Picture Prompt: ‘Our Bright Blue Planet’
item.description: Have you seen this image this week? What is your reaction?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: Finding Trends in Sports Stats and ‘Wheel of Fortune’ Puzzles
item.description: Ben Blatt, a data reporter for The Upshot, dreams up inventive ways to answer questions like: “Is Taylor Swift actually increasing N.F.L. ratings?”
item.dc:creator: Sarah Bahr

item.title: NYT Spelling Bee Hints for April 7, 2026
item.description: Feeling stuck on today’s puzzle? We can help.
item.dc:creator: New York Times Games

item.title: Stephen Colbert Mocks Trump’s Expletive-Filled Post Threatening Iran
item.description: Late night hosts mocked the president’s expletive-filled ultimatum to Iran, posted on Easter Sunday morning.
item.dc:creator: Trish Bendix
item.category: Late Show with Stephen Colbert (TV Program)

item.title: Oil Prices Jump as Trump’s Deadline for Deal Draws Near
item.description: President Trump's Tuesday evening deadline for a deal approached after he rejected a cease-fire proposal as “not good enough.”
item.dc:creator: The New York Times
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Cómo la IA ayudó a un hombre (y a su hermano) a crear una empresa de 1800 millones de dólares
item.description: ¿Quién necesita más de dos empleados cuando la inteligencia artificial puede hacer tantas tareas empresariales? Es súper eficaz… y un poco solitario.
item.dc:creator: Erin Griffith

media:content.media:credit: Maggie Shannon para The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Para construir Medvi, Gallagher utilizó herramientas de IA como ChatGPT, Claude, Grok, Midjourney y Runway.

item.title: Word of the Day: contiguous
item.description: This word has appeared in 44 articles on NYTimes.com in the past year. Can you use it in a sentence?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: Una forma sencilla de hacer tu entrenamiento más intenso
item.description: Si tu rutina de ejercicio te parece aburrida, prueba a añadir un poco de altura a tus ejercicios habituales.
item.dc:creator: Jen Murphy and Theodore Tae

item.title: TK
item.description: TK

item.title: How Much Humiliation Can JD Vance Take?
item.description: The vice president gets saddled with the Iran war and eroding popularity.
item.dc:creator: Dana Milbank
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Ioulex for The New York Times

item.title: The Fire in the West Bank Is Burning Hotter, Out of Sight
item.description: An Israeli law aiming capital punishment at Palestinians but not Jewish settlers is immoral, unconstitutional and part of a larger effort to suppress rights.
item.dc:creator: Talia Sasson
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: TK
item.description: TK

item.title: Trump Calls Artemis II Astronauts After Their Historic Journey Around the Moon
item.description: The conversation celebrated a small, but significant, step in an ambitious plan for missions to the moon and Mars that Mr. Trump had set early in his first term.
item.dc:creator: Chris Cameron

media:content.media:description: Artemis II astronauts speaking with President Trump from their Orion spacecraft on Monday.

item.title: Vance to Visit Hungary to Boost Orban Before Election
item.description: A scheduled visit by the American vice president, JD Vance, makes clear that Russia is not the only country invested in a victory for Hungary’s leader, Viktor Orban.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Higgins

media:content.media:description: Prime Minister Viktor Orban at a campaign rally in Kaposvar, Hungary, in March.

item.title: Why Hungary’s Election Could Swing on Roma Votes
item.description: Prime Minister Viktor Orban’s policies affecting the Roma minority have put those voters in play in upcoming parliamentary elections. In a tight race, they could make the difference.
item.dc:creator: Lara Jakes and Mate Halmos

media:content.media:credit: Tamas Paczai for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Roma and non-Roma children at a mixed kindergarten in Kalocsa, Hungary.

item.title: In Paris’s Catacombs, Can a Restoration Breathe New Life Into City’s Dead?
item.description: For centuries, the bones of some six million people were buried in the catacombs beneath the city. Curators are trying to preserve and modernize the tunnels while maintaining the spooky ambience.
item.dc:creator: Ana Castelain

media:content.media:credit: Dmitry Kostyukov for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Workers restoring the catacombs in Paris last month.

item.title: Quote of the Day: Artemis II Astronauts Reach New Frontier in Human Travel
item.description: Quotation of the Day for Tuesday, April 7, 2026.

item.title: Offset, Former Migos Rapper, Is Shot Outside Florida Casino
item.description: The former member of the chart-topping Atlanta trio was in stable condition after being shot in Hollywood, Fla., his representative said. The police said they had detained two people.
item.dc:creator: Francesca Regalado

media:content.media:credit: Peyton Fulford for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The rapper Offset was injured in a shooting in Hollywood, Fla., on Monday.

item.title: NYT Crossword Answers for April 7, 2026
item.description: This debut from Adryel W. Robles Ojeda will set you free.
item.dc:creator: Madison Malone Kircher

media:content.media:description: Pipe down! We’ve got puzzles to solve.

item.title: ‘Becky Shaw’ Review: Funny vs. Likable? Funny Wins.
item.description: Alden Ehrenreich makes a show-stealing Broadway debut in Gina Gionfriddo’s comedy about two old friends, one disastrous blind date and the dicey aftermath.
item.dc:creator: Laura Collins-Hughes

media:content.media:credit: Rachel Papo for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: In “Becky Shaw,” the characters played by Madeline Brewer, left, and Alden Ehrenreich are set up on a blind date.

item.title: How Trump’s Endorsement in California Could Backfire Against Republicans
item.description: President Trump endorsed Steve Hilton, a Republican, in the governor’s race, which could help Democrats avoid being shut out of the general election.
item.dc:creator: Laurel Rosenhall

media:content.media:description: Steve Hilton, a Republican and former Fox News host, was endorsed by President Trump in the California governor’s race.

item.title: La tripulación de Artemis II inicia su regreso a la Tierra y presenció un eclipse solar
item.description: Desde la cara oculta de la Luna, los astronautas del Integrity observaron la actividad del Sol durante un eclipse total solar desde el punto de vista de la nave.
item.dc:creator: Katrina Miller

media:content.media:credit: Ashley L. Conti para The New York Times
media:content.media:description: La tripulación del Artemis II tendrá la oportunidad de ver un eclipse solar, pero puede que no se parezca al gran eclipse americano de abril de 2024.

item.title: Corrections: April 7, 2026
item.description: Corrections that appeared in print on Tuesday, April 7, 2026.

item.title: Mamdani Celebrates Passover at Progressive ‘Seder in the Street’
item.description: The mayor has observed the Passover holiday in several ways in recent days, including a left-wing event on Monday. He planned to hold a Seder for city workers in the evening at Gracie Mansion.
item.dc:creator: Emma Goldberg
item.category: Jews for Racial and Economic Justice

media:content.media:description: Mayor Zohran Mamdani told attendees at a progressive Passover event that the holiday included “lessons of hope, overcoming fear.”

item.title: Whale That Swam 20 Miles Up Washington River Is Found Dead
item.description: The gray whale, which some locals affectionately named Willapa Willy, was found on Saturday afternoon after first being spotted swimming up the Willapa River last week.
item.dc:creator: Livia Albeck-Ripka

media:content.media:credit: Cascadia Research Collective, via Associated Press
media:content.media:description: A gray whale swimming in the Willapa River near Willapa Bay, Wash., on Wednesday.

item.title: Trump reitera sus amenazas a Irán aun con negociaciones en marcha
item.description: Una contrapropuesta de Irán de 10 puntos para poner fin a la guerra representa una posible salida. El presidente Trump la calificó como un paso significativo, pero “no suficiente”.
item.dc:creator: Zolan Kanno-Youngs, Erika Solomon, Aaron Boxerman and Michael Levenson
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi para The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Un retrato del difunto líder supremo de Irán, el ayatolá Alí Jameneí, en Teherán el lunes.

item.title: Hegseth Likens Easter Rescue of U.S. Airman to Resurrection of Jesus Christ
item.description: President Trump also asserted that God supports the American war against Iran “because God is good, and God wants to see people taken care of.”
item.dc:creator: Chris Cameron
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth speaking at a news conference at the White House on Monday.

item.title: Trump Threatens Jail if Journalists Protect Certain Iran Sources
item.description: The president indicated he would ask an unnamed media outlet to reveal the sources behind its coverage of Iran’s successful strike on a U.S. fighter jet, and of its crew.
item.dc:creator: Erik Wemple

media:content.media:description: President Trump at a news conference in Washington on Monday.

item.title: Iconic Fashion Shows Where Flowers and Plants Were the Stars
item.description: With these mostly spring presentations, the designers went deep on their love of flowers and plants.
item.dc:creator: Jameson Montgomery

media:content.media:description: Alexander McQueen spring 2007

item.title: Here’s What Happened in the War in Iran and the Middle East on Monday
item.description: President Trump escalated his threats ahead of his pending Tuesday deadline for Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz.
item.dc:creator: Ephrat Livni
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Avishag Shaar-Yashuv for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Inspecting damages on Monday inside a library of an ultra-Orthodox girls’ school following a missile strike in Tel Aviv.

item.title: The Dark Side of the Moon Is Really the Far Side
item.description: Scientists prefer to call the backside of the moon its “far” side.
item.dc:creator: Andrea Kannapell

media:content.media:credit: Cassandra Klos for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The moon’s backside is lit by the sun, but out of reach of human vision from our planet’s surface.

item.title: La guerra está convirtiendo a Irán en una potencia mundial
item.description: Su nuevo poder deriva de su control del estrecho de Ormuz.
item.dc:creator: Robert A. Pape
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Ilustración fotográfica de Tam Stockton para The New York Times

item.title: Student News Quiz for March 31-April 6
item.description: Have you been paying attention to current events recently? See how many of these 10 questions you can get right.
item.dc:creator: Michael Gonchar

item.title: Trump Says Iran Proposal Isn’t Enough to Stop Attacks on Bridges and Power Plants
item.description: President Trump has told Iran it must open the Strait of Hormuz by 8 p.m. Tuesday or face the consequences, although he has delayed previous deadlines.
item.dc:creator: Tyler Pager and Erika Solomon
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Damaged buildings in Tehran. President Trump said on Monday that the United States would consider helping to rebuild Iran if they can strike a deal.

item.title: Trump Escalates Threats to Devastate Iran
item.description: President Trump on Monday renewed his threat to attack Iran if the country does not agree to a deal to reopen the Strait of Hormuz by 8 p.m. Eastern time on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Cynthia Silva and Daniel Fetherston

item.title: How to Follow NASA’s Artemis II Around the Far Side of the Moon
item.description: The astronauts will head into a communications blackout at 6:44 p.m. Eastern time as they become the first people to travel around the moon since 1972.
item.dc:creator: Anushka Patil

media:content.media:credit: Cassandra Klos for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: NASA’s Lunar Science Team monitoring a topographical map of the moon on Monday in the Science Evaluation Room at Johnson Space Center in Houston.

item.title: A Redistricting War in Florida, Georgia’s Runoff and the Latest Politics News
item.description: As deadlines approach in the next two weeks, neither is going quite according to the partisan plan.
item.dc:creator: Nick Corasaniti and Patricia Mazzei

media:content.media:description: Gov. Ron DeSantis of Florida has indicated that he plans to continue with the push to draw new maps.

item.title: Los familiares de la tripulación de Artemis II también son parte de la misión
item.description: Mucho antes de que se produzca el despegue, no solo los astronautas sienten la carga de tal riesgo, sino también sus seres queridos.
item.dc:creator: Katrina Miller

media:content.media:description: Familiares de astronautas y otras personas —con Pat Collins, a la izquierda, esposa del astronauta Michael Collins— viendo la cobertura televisiva del Apolo 11 el 20 de julio de 1969

item.title: Artemis II Astronauts Go Farther From Earth Than Ever Before
item.description: Also, Trump threatens to attack Iran’s power plants and bridges. Here’s the latest at the end of Monday.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Cullen

media:content.media:description: Reid Wiseman looks at the Earth on Thursday.

item.title: 6 Takeaways From Trump’s News Conference on Iran
item.description: President Trump described the risky mission to rescue an Air Force colonel whose fighter jet had been shot down, but he offered no clear path out of the war.
item.dc:creator: Anton Troianovski and Luke Broadwater
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:description: “We have to have a deal that’s acceptable to me,” President Trump said on Monday. “And part of that deal is going to be we want free traffic of oil and everything else.”

item.title: Map: Minor Earthquake Strikes Near San Diego
item.description: View the location of the quake’s epicenter and shake area.
item.dc:creator: William B. Davis, Madison Dong, Judson Jones, John Keefe, Joey K. Lee and Bea Malsky

item.title: Watch Live: Artemis II Mission
item.description: The four Artemis astronauts will pass behind the far side of the moon, seeing parts of the moon never observed with human eyes.

item.title: Artemis Astronauts Set Record for Farthest Distance Traveled From Earth
item.description: The Artemis II astronauts ventured farther from Earth than anyone else in the history of humanity on Monday, breaking the record set by Apollo 13 in 1970.
item.dc:creator: Jackeline Luna

item.title: Highlights from Artemis II’s Historic Journey Around the Moon
item.description: As they traveled back toward Earth following a solar eclipse and a communications blackout, President Trump called the space capsule to praise the four astronauts on their success.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times
